name: Harassment (Civil) Action Checklist
description: Action steps for filing civil case for harassment

base_steps.action: Document all harassment incidents with evidence
details: Create detailed written record of each harassment incident with date, time, location, and witnesses
Save all communications (emails, messages, letters) from harasser
Take screenshots of harassment messages, social media posts, etc.
Keep medical reports if there are any health impacts
Store evidence securely in multiple locations (cloud + physical)
Schedule consultation with lawyer experienced in harassment cases
Bring all evidence and documentation
Discuss options: civil suit, tort claim, compensation amount
Understand costs, timeline, and likely outcomes
Clarify difference between civil and criminal remedies

base_steps.action: Send formal cease and desist notice
details: Draft notice with lawyer's assistance
Send notice via registered mail/email with acknowledgment
Give reasonable deadline for response (typically 2-4 weeks)
Keep all correspondence and delivery proof
Get acknowledgment of receipt

base_steps.action: File civil suit for damages if needed
details: If harassment continues after notice, file civil suit
Sue for general damages, special damages, and mental anguish
Request injunction to prevent future harassment
File in appropriate civil court (district court or high court)
Pay court filing fees

base_steps.action: Attend court hearings and comply with court orders
details: Never miss scheduled hearings
Bring all evidence and witnesses
Follow all court orders and directions
Document all new harassment incidents
Keep lawyer informed of any updates
Apply for interim injunction to stop harassment immediately
Request protection order if in danger
Ask for compensation during pendency of case
Attend all hearings for injunction
